remaining_tasks.name: Manual UAT of /lz-advisor.execute on plugin 0.8.9 against the plan from uat-plan-skill-fixes (Phase 7 Finding A and B regression check; commit 2173e39)
instrumented_observations: Phase 7 Finding A repro check: does the execute-skill executor silently apply-then-revert any advisor Critical, or now issue a reconciliation call?

Phase 7 Finding B.1 repro check: does the execute-skill carry the plan-skill's pv-* blocks into its consultations? (Note: plan-fixes plan output has 0 pv-* blocks, so test is whether executor recognizes the missing anchors and either resolves them empirically or surfaces the gap)

Phase 7 Finding C repro check: does the execute-skill catch the unanchored Storybook 8+ claim from the plan and either verify it empirically (web search for @storybook/angular@10.3.5 fn() integration) or surface the version-claim contradiction?
Phase 6 Pattern D regression check: when input is a plan file with no pv-* blocks AND the contested fn() question, does the execute-skill orient phase fire WebSearch / WebFetch?
Whether the fn() spy fix (4 of 5 consultations now recommend it) is shipped without being silent-reverted again

remaining_tasks.name: Manual UAT of /lz-advisor.security-review on plugin 0.8.9 against the post-fix state (commit range from uat/manual-s4-v089-compodoc base through the fix commits)

human_actions_pending.action: Launch interactive Claude in ngx-smart-components testbed and run /lz-advisor.execute on the plan from uat-plan-skill-fixes (commit 2173e39)
human_actions_pending.context: Implements the 5-step plan in `plans/address-review-findings-compodoc-storybook.plan.md`. Tests Phase 7 Findings A, B.1, B.2, and C as regression / repro checks. The plan output has zero pv-* blocks and an unanchored 'canonical Storybook 8+' qualifier; whether the executor empirically grounds these claims (especially fn() spy + version mismatch with installed 10.3.5) before applying determines whether the confidence-laundering chain extends to hop 6 (committed source code). Use canonical prompt format: '/lz-advisor.execute Implement the plan at @plans/address-review-findings-compodoc-storybook.plan.md.' Branch is uat/manual-s4-v089-compodoc.

decisions.decision: Path C2 (descriptive-trigger refinement) chosen over C1 (advisor refusal) and C3 (PreToolUse hook)
decisions.rationale: Anthropic 2026 prompt-engineering doc and prior memory both warn against imperative escalation; descriptive triggers + worked examples + reordering + tightened triggers is the recommended pattern.

decisions.decision: Strip personal CLAUDE.md fetch chain references (markdown.new, url-to-markdown, playwright-cli) from SKILL body
decisions.rationale: Marketplace plugin neutrality. User-level fetch substitutes operate at a different precedence layer; plugin shouldn't enumerate them.

decisions.decision: Treat Stage 1 KCB synthetic-fixture FAIL as historical context, not operative gate verdict
decisions.rationale: Manual UAT on plugin 0.8.9 against realistic Compodoc + Storybook setup prompt produced 7 web tool calls and the highest-quality plan of any version tested. KCB synthetic prompt didn't exercise the rich orient + pre_verified pattern.

decisions.decision: Update 06-VERIFICATION.md to PASS-with-caveat (gate_verdict, status, web_usage_count) and append amendment section
decisions.rationale: Reflects empirical state on plugin 0.8.9 while preserving Stage 1 history for traceability.

decisions.decision: Broaden Phase 7 Finding B from intra-skill carry-forward to cross-skill carry-forward
decisions.rationale: Review-skill UAT showed pv-* blocks were not packaged at all in the review consultation, even though plan skill produced 4 anchored claims. The fix surface is the workflow boundary, not just a single skill's Verification template.

decisions.decision: Add a plan+execute fix cycle UAT before security-review and use it as a regression check for Phase 7 Findings A and B
decisions.rationale: The contested fn() spy question deserves empirical resolution via plan-skill web research before any code change. Running plan+execute on the review findings doubles as a Phase 7 repro check: does executor silent-revert recur, do plan-skill pv-* blocks carry forward into execute-skill consultations? Two new behavioral data points for ~$2 of plugin spend.

decisions.decision: Capture Pattern D suppression on review-file authoritative-source treatment as Phase 6 gap (06-VERIFICATION.md second amendment) rather than Phase 7 candidate
decisions.rationale: Phase 6's scope is question-class-aware orient ranking (Pattern D). The plan-fixes UAT showed Pattern D's web-first prescription is being suppressed when input is review output shaped like authoritative source. The fix surface is `<context_trust_contract>` in the SKILL.md files -- exactly Phase 6's surface. Phase 7's confidence-laundering and pv-* discipline gaps compound this but the root cause is the trust-contract carve-out.

decisions.decision: Sharpen Phase 7 Finding B into B.1 (carry-forward) + B.2 (Pre-verified Claims confabulation) and add new Finding C (4-hop confidence-laundering chain)
decisions.rationale: Plan-fixes UAT exposed two distinct failure modes that Phase 7 Finding B's original framing combined imprecisely. B.1 is the carry-forward gap (existing). B.2 is the pre-verified-header-confabulation gap (NEW from plan-fixes UAT, including the hallucinated 'haiku' import). C is the cross-skill propagation chain that makes A + B compounding.

next_action: Launch /lz-advisor.execute UAT in ngx-smart-components testbed against the plan-fixes plan (commit 2173e39, file plans/address-review-findings-compodoc-storybook.plan.md). Plan output has zero pv-* blocks and an unanchored Storybook 8+ qualifier; the execute UAT tests how the executor handles inherited unverified claims and whether the confidence-laundering chain extends to source code. Invoke as: '/lz-advisor.execute Implement the plan at @plans/address-review-findings-compodoc-storybook.plan.md.'
context_notes: Phase 6 has now closed plan-skill, execute-skill, review-skill, and plan-fixes UATs on plugin 0.8.9. The plan-fixes UAT verdict was PASS-with-significant-observations: surfaced one new Phase 6 gap (Pattern D suppression on review-file authoritative-source treatment, captured as 06-VERIFICATION.md second amendment), sharpened Phase 7 Finding B with B.2 sub-issue (Pre-verified Claims header confabulation including hallucinated `haiku` import), and added new Phase 7 Finding C (4-hop confidence-laundering chain). The execute-fixes UAT now has multiple regression checks layered on the same execution: Phase 7 Finding A (silent apply-then-revert), Finding B.1 (pv-* carry-forward; trivially confirmed since plan has 0 pv-*), Finding B.2 (does executor recognize the plan's mislabeled pre-verified content as suspect?), Finding C (does executor catch Storybook 8+ claim and verify against installed 10.3.5?), plus the Phase 6 Pattern D regression on plan-file input.

uat_iteration_history.change: Promoted WebSearch-first to SKILL body + Bash-substitute prohibition
Narrowed prohibition to WebSearch-only (allow fetch substitutes)
Reordered ranking to web-first + tightened step 2 trigger + worked example + stripped personal-tool refs
uat_iteration_history.result: 2 WebSearch + 5 WebFetch + 4 pre_verified blocks; highest-quality plan
